meta.name: TruthLens Trusted Sources Reference Database
meta.description: A structured reference database of trusted, credible, and known-unreliable news and information sources. Used by TruthLens to assess source credibility signals when users paste articles or links. Sources are categorized by type, region, topic, and reliability tier.
meta.project: TruthLens — DTI Subject Project · AI & Society

reliability_tiers.TIER_1: Highly credible — peer-reviewed, government statistics, major wire services with strong editorial standards
reliability_tiers.TIER_2: Generally credible — established news organizations with editorial oversight
reliability_tiers.TIER_3: Mixed credibility — partisan outlets, opinion-heavy sources, requires verification
reliability_tiers.TIER_4: Low credibility — known misinformation, satire (often mislabeled), conspiracy sites

scientific_and_research.name: Nature
scientific_and_research.notes: One of the world's most prestigious multidisciplinary scientific journals. Rigorous peer review.

scientific_and_research.name: The Lancet
scientific_and_research.notes: Top-tier medical journal. Note: The 1998 Wakefield autism-vaccine paper was published here but later fully retracted — demonstrates importance of post-publication peer review.

scientific_and_research.name: Science
scientific_and_research.notes: Published by the American Association for the Advancement of Science. Exceptionally rigorous.

scientific_and_research.name: PLOS ONE
scientific_and_research.notes: Open-access peer-reviewed journal. Lower barrier to entry than Nature/Science but still peer-reviewed.

scientific_and_research.name: PubMed / NCBI
scientific_and_research.notes: Index of biomedical literature maintained by the U.S. National Library of Medicine. Credibility of individual papers varies, but preprint vs published status is clearly labeled.

scientific_and_research.name: arXiv
scientific_and_research.notes: Preprint server — papers are NOT peer reviewed. Widely used in physics and CS. Always check if paper has been formally published.

international_wire_services.name: Reuters
international_wire_services.notes: One of the world's most reliable news wires. Strong fact-checking standards. Reuters Fact Check is a separate dedicated unit.

international_wire_services.name: Associated Press (AP)
international_wire_services.notes: Not-for-profit wire service. AP Fact Check is a dedicated unit. Very high editorial standards.

international_wire_services.name: Agence France-Presse (AFP)
international_wire_services.notes: Global news agency. AFP Fact Check is highly regarded internationally.

international_wire_services.name: Press Trust of India (PTI)
international_wire_services.notes: India's primary domestic news wire. Generally reliable for factual news reporting.

international_wire_services.name: ANI (Asian News International)
international_wire_services.notes: Widely used Indian wire service. Generally factual but has faced criticism for occasionally publishing government press releases without adequate scrutiny.

global_news_organizations.name: BBC News
global_news_organizations.notes: Public broadcaster with strong editorial charter and BBC Verify fact-checking unit. One of the world's most trusted sources.

global_news_organizations.name: The Guardian
global_news_organizations.notes: High-quality journalism with a liberal-leaning editorial perspective. News section is reliable; opinion section is clearly labeled.

global_news_organizations.name: The New York Times
global_news_organizations.notes: High editorial standards. Has faced occasional criticism for specific stories. News and opinion sections clearly differentiated.

global_news_organizations.name: The Wall Street Journal
global_news_organizations.notes: Highly credible news section. Opinion section has a distinct right-of-center perspective.

global_news_organizations.name: Al Jazeera English
global_news_organizations.notes: Strong international reporting, particularly in regions underrepresented by Western media. Funded by Qatari government — evaluate geopolitical coverage accordingly.

indian_news_organizations.name: The Hindu
indian_news_organizations.notes: One of India's most respected newspapers. Strong fact-checking culture. English-language.

indian_news_organizations.name: The Indian Express
indian_news_organizations.notes: High editorial standards. Noted for investigative journalism. Has a dedicated Fact Check section.

indian_news_organizations.name: Hindustan Times
indian_news_organizations.notes: Major English-language daily. Generally reliable for factual reporting.

indian_news_organizations.name: NDTV
indian_news_organizations.notes: Established Indian television news channel. Factual reporting standards are generally high.

indian_news_organizations.name: The Wire
indian_news_organizations.notes: Independent investigative journalism. Left-of-center perspective. Strong on accountability reporting, particularly regarding government. Has had some sourcing controversies.

indian_news_organizations.name: Scroll.in
indian_news_organizations.notes: Digital news outlet with strong fact-checking culture. Generally reliable.

indian_news_organizations.name: Alt News
indian_news_organizations.notes: India's most prominent independent fact-checking organization. IFCN-certified. Specializes in debunking viral misinformation in Indian media.

indian_news_organizations.name: Boom Live
indian_news_organizations.notes: IFCN-certified Indian fact-checker. Covers misinformation in Hindi and English.

indian_news_organizations.name: Vishvas News
indian_news_organizations.notes: IFCN-certified multilingual fact-checker covering Hindi, Punjabi, and other Indian languages.

fact_checking_organizations.name: Snopes
fact_checking_organizations.notes: One of the original fact-checking websites. Independently run, IFCN-certified. Strong on viral social media claims.

fact_checking_organizations.name: PolitiFact
fact_checking_organizations.notes: Pulitzer Prize-winning political fact-checker. Uses 'Truth-O-Meter' scale. IFCN-certified.

fact_checking_organizations.name: FactCheck.org
fact_checking_organizations.notes: Operated by the Annenberg Public Policy Center. Non-partisan. IFCN-certified.

fact_checking_organizations.name: Reuters Fact Check
fact_checking_organizations.notes: Dedicated fact-checking unit within Reuters. IFCN-certified. Strong international coverage.

fact_checking_organizations.name: AFP Fact Check
fact_checking_organizations.notes: AFP's global fact-checking unit. Available in multiple languages. IFCN-certified.

fact_checking_organizations.name: WHO Mythbusters
fact_checking_organizations.notes: WHO's dedicated misinformation debunking page. Primary source for global public health claims.

fact_checking_organizations.name: Full Fact (UK)
fact_checking_organizations.notes: UK's leading independent fact-checking charity. IFCN-certified.

official_government_and_statistical_sour.name: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) — India
official_government_and_statistical_sour.notes: Primary source for India's official macroeconomic statistics including CPI, GDP, and employment data.

official_government_and_statistical_sour.name: Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
official_government_and_statistical_sour.notes: India's central bank. Primary source for monetary policy decisions, repo rate, and financial system data.

official_government_and_statistical_sour.name: Press Information Bureau (PIB) Fact Check
official_government_and_statistical_sour.notes: Government's own fact-checking unit. Reliable for debunking false claims about government policies, but inherently limited to government-favored framing.

official_government_and_statistical_sour.name: NASA
official_government_and_statistical_sour.notes: Primary source for US space exploration news and NASA climate data.

official_government_and_statistical_sour.name: NOAA (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration)
official_government_and_statistical_sour.notes: Primary source for US and global climate and weather data.

official_government_and_statistical_sour.name: ISRO
official_government_and_statistical_sour.notes: Indian Space Research Organisation. Primary source for Indian space missions.

official_government_and_statistical_sour.name: CDC (Centers for Disease Control and Prevention)
official_government_and_statistical_sour.notes: Primary US public health authority. Authoritative source on vaccines, disease data, and health guidelines.

international_organizations.name: World Health Organization (WHO)
international_organizations.notes: Global health authority. Primary source for international public health data and recommendations.

international_organizations.name: IPCC (Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change)
international_organizations.notes: The gold standard for climate science. Reports synthesize thousands of peer-reviewed studies reviewed by hundreds of scientists.

international_organizations.name: World Economic Forum (WEF)
international_organizations.notes: Produces research reports (e.g., Future of Jobs) that are widely cited. Reports are genuine research but reflect a pro-globalization perspective.

international_organizations.name: World Bank
international_organizations.notes: Primary source for global poverty, economic development, and development statistics.

international_organizations.name: IRENA (International Renewable Energy Agency)
international_organizations.notes: Primary source for global renewable energy capacity data (solar, wind, etc.).

low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.name: Natural News
low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.notes: Repeatedly flagged by fact-checkers for publishing dangerous health misinformation, anti-vaccine content, and conspiracy theories. Banned from Facebook and Google News.

low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.name: InfoWars
low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.notes: Operated by Alex Jones. Consistently produces false and harmful misinformation. Multiple platform bans. Owner has faced legal consequences for false claims.

low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.name: The Epoch Times
low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.notes: Linked to Falun Gong. Has published COVID-19 misinformation and was the largest funder of pro-Trump Facebook advertising. Consistently rated as low-credibility by media watchdogs.

low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.name: The Onion
low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.notes: Deliberate satire. Labeled as such. However, Onion headlines frequently circulate without the satire label and are mistaken for real news.

low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.name: Fauxy.com (India)
low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.notes: Indian satirical news site. Similar to The Onion. Frequently mistaken for real news when headlines are shared without context.

low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.name: Postcard News (India)
low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.notes: Multiple fact-checking organizations have documented consistent false and communally inflammatory content. Several cases referred to police.

low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.name: OpIndia
low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.notes: Strong right-wing perspective. Mixes genuine news with partisan framing. Fact-checkers have flagged numerous inaccuracies, but not all content is false.

low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.name: WhatsApp Forwards (generic)
low_credibility_and_known_misinformation.notes: WhatsApp forwards are a primary vector for misinformation in India, particularly in regional languages. Should always be independently verified before being trusted or shared.

detection_signals_reference.description: Common linguistic and structural signals used by TruthLens to assess credibility. Not definitive on their own — used in combination.
high_risk_signals: ALL CAPS words or excessive exclamation marks
Anonymous sources ('a source close to...', 'whistleblower reveals...')
Conspiracy framing ('they don’t want you to know', 'mainstream media is hiding')
Urgent sharing prompts ('share before they delete this')
Suppression narratives ('doctors hate him', 'Big Pharma is hiding')
Vague authoritative attribution ('experts say', 'scientists confirm' without names)
Claims that contradict established scientific consensus without peer-reviewed evidence
No named publication, institution, or author
Emotionally charged language designed to provoke fear or outrage
positive_credibility_signals: Conditional and hedged language for uncertain claims
Corrections policy visible on source website
Story corroborated by wire services (Reuters, AP, AFP)
